goals.g1: Does resolve_compound() correctly implement Compound Match Resolution step 2 (rank overlaps by degree, resolve inherit-collisions to forced-born, redirect boundary crossings to the owning match)?

c1.statement: for overlapping_nodes, resolve_compound picks a deterministic winner (lowest match index) to keep inherit rights and forces every other match on that node to mint fresh (born) for its own output instead -- an override layered on the compiled EditsArtifact, itself untouched

c2.statement: for boundary_crossings, resolve_compound redirects match i's crossing out_node to resolve through owning match j's output identity at apply time, found by matching the actual live crossing target against the overlap's node set, not by re-checking binding[in_node]

c3.statement: test 1 (overlapping_nodes) produced forced_born={1:{b_input}}, redirects={}, resolved_owner={r2:0} -- correct

c4.statement: test 2 (boundary_crossings) produced forced_born={}, redirects={(0,out1):1}, resolved_owner={} -- correct

c5.statement: the two test outcomes show no cross-contamination between the two resolution branches

c6.statement: ranking-by-degree currently only affects iteration order not outcome, because neither test scenario exercises a chained/transitive overlap -- cascade behavior is unverified past this depth

c7.statement: DECISION: resolve_compound correctly resolves both overlap cases at the depth tested (2 matches, 1 overlap node each), producing forced_born/redirects/resolved_owner an apply-time orchestrator needs. Transitive/cascading multi-node overlaps are explicitly UNVERIFIED and carried forward as an open risk before this is used on real multi-rule firings.

derived_from.rule: both classification branches verified independently and cleanly separated; cascade scope explicitly bounded, not silently assumed safe

ans.statement: resolve_compound correctly resolves both overlap cases at the depth tested (2 matches, 1 overlap node each), producing forced_born/redirects/resolved_owner an apply-time orchestrator needs. Transitive/cascading multi-node overlaps are explicitly UNVERIFIED and carried forward as an open risk before this is used on real multi-rule firings.

k1.method: ran both hand-built scenarios and inspected forced_born/redirects/resolved_owner directly against hand-predicted values before execution
k1.result: test1 and test2 both matched predictions exactly, no branch produced output in the other's territory

k2.method: checked whether either test scenario contained a third match or a node overlapping two distinct already-resolved nodes
k2.result: neither test constructed a chained/cascading case -- c6 is a scope limitation, not a verified property

committed.statement: DECISION: resolve_compound correctly resolves both overlap cases at the depth tested (2 matches, 1 overlap node each), producing forced_born/redirects/resolved_owner an apply-time orchestrator needs. Transitive/cascading multi-node overlaps are explicitly UNVERIFIED and carried forward as an open risk before this is used on real multi-rule firings.
